'''Tom Davies''' (born 11 January 1997) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[2017 RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Davies was born in [[Wigan]], [[Lancashire, England]].

He signed for Wigan after an initial trial having played amateur rugby league for Shevington Sharks, Orrell St. James and [[Wigan St Patricks|Wigan St. Patrick’s]]. At the time of the trial he was playing semi-professional rugby union with Fylde after being released from a scholarship with [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Wigan's Tom Davies: 'I came to terms with the fact I wasn't going to make it' |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/feb/16/wigan-tom-davies-world-club-challenge-sydney-preview |work=The Guardian |accessdate=21 February 2019}}</ref> He made his professional début on 19 March 2017, scoring a try against [[Huddersfield Giants]] in a 16–16 draw.<ref>[http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wigan-vs-huddersfield/46020 Wigan 16-16 Huddersfield: Giants hold Warriors at DW Stadium]</ref>

== Club career ==
===Wigan Warriors===
Following the departure of [[Josh Charnley]] to the [[Sale Sharks]], and injuries to first team wingers [[Joe Burgess]], [[Lewis Tierney]] and [[Dominic Manfredi|Dom Manfredi]], Davies got his début against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. After scoring on début he kept his place despite the return of [[Joe Burgess|Burgess]] and [[Lewis Tierney|Tierney]]. He then scored a brace of long range tries against the [[Catalans Dragons]]. Further tries against [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] helped him to a total of 14 tries in his rookie season.{{citation needed|date=January 2020}}

After an impressive rookie season, Davies was handed the number 2 shirt at the [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] club replacing the injured [[Dominic Manfredi]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/news/2018-01-16-2018-squad-numbers|title=2018 Squad Numbers {{!}} News {{!}} Wigan Warriors|website=www.wiganwarriors.com|access-date=2018-01-16}}</ref> Davies scored Wigan's first try of the year with a diving effort against the [[Salford Red Devils]] followed by further tries against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] followed by a brace against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] to give him five tries in six appearances. His next try came four games later against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Magic Weekend]] after [[Morgan Escaré|Morgan Escare]] scooped up a loose pass and selflessly passed the ball to Davies rather than running to the line himself. Further tries came against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], Warrington, Huddersfield, Wakefield and a 50m solo run against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. After four games without scoring he scored a try in the derby against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] from a seemingly impossible position with two tacklers holding him up 2m from the line, but drove forward and reached out to plant the ball on the white line just after halftime to halt a potential Saints fightback. Known for his aggressive ball carrying he averaged 148m per game as well as averaging 3 tackle breaks and a clean break per match. Following his early season form he was called up to the [[England Knights]] Performance Squad<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wiganwarriors.com/news/2018-03-09-qa-with-tom-davies|title=Q&A with Tom Davies {{!}} News {{!}} Wigan Warriors|website=www.wiganwarriors.com|access-date=2018-09-21}}</ref> before signing a new five year deal with the [[Wigan Warriors]] just a year after making his debut despite only recently signing a new improved deal.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43823550|title=Tom Davies: Wigan Warriors winger signs new five-year deal|date=2018-04-19|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2018-09-21|language=en-GB}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
In round 5 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored a [[hat-trick]] for Catalans in their victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/tom-davies-hat-trick-and-dean-whare-double-helps-catalans-cruise-past-wakefield/|title=Catalans v Wakefield Trinity match report|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>
On 9 October, Davies played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, Davies played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Davies was sent to the sin bin during the second half of the match for a professional foul.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 5 June 2024 it was reported that he had signed for Hull Kingston Rovers in the Super League on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=5 Jun 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=England winger Davies to join Hull KR from Catalans|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm55dxpp5m1o}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Davies played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 2025 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years. Davies scored the winning try with less than two minutes remaining.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 18 September 2025, Davies played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Final game of the regular season in victory over Warrington to claim the League Leaders Shield <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Davies played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 19 February 2026, Davies played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref> Davies also played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|PNG]] at the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref>

On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he made a try-scoring début for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in their 24-26 defeat to the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England’s [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=25 Jun 2021|accessdate=26 Jun 2021|title=England 24-26 Combined All Stars - Shaun Wane loses first game|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57618533}}</ref>

He scored his second try for England in the 10-30 win over [[France national rugby league team|France]] on [[International rugby league in 2021|23 Oct 2021]], staged in [[Perpignan]].
